UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a player's core profile and account information within Axiom Frontier. This entity is central for player management and links to their in-game activities and owned agents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: The player's chosen username, unique across the game.

email.description: The player's email address, used for communication and account recovery.

registrationDate.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

lastLoginDate.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

totalPlaytimeSeconds.description: Accumulated total playtime of the user in seconds.

adminNotes.description: Internal notes added by administrators regarding this user profile.

GameWorldSetting.title: GameWorldSetting
GameWorldSetting.description: Stores global configuration parameters and settings for the game world, manageable via the Admin Console. This allows dynamic adjustments to game mechanics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameWorldSetting entity (e.g., a setting key name).

settingName.description: A descriptive, unique name for the game world setting.

settingValue.description: The value of the setting, stored as a string to accommodate various data types (numbers, booleans, JSON objects) which can be parsed as needed.

description.description: A detailed explanation of what this game setting controls.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of when this setting was last modified.

PlayerAgent.title: PlayerAgent
PlayerAgent.description: Represents an individual autonomous agent controlled by a player within the game world, capable of performing various actions like gathering, combat, or trading.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerAgent entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that owns this agent. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PlayerAgent)

name.description: The name of the player agent.

currentLocation.description: The current geographical or logical location of the agent within the game world.

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

energy.description: The current energy points of the agent, used for actions.

experiencePoints.description: The accumulated experience points of the agent.

inventoryItemIds.description: References to GameItems currently in the agent's inventory. (Relationship: GameItem N:N PlayerAgent)

assignedQuestIds.description: References to Quests currently assigned to or being undertaken by this agent. (Relationship: Quest N:N PlayerAgent)

behaviorState.description: The current behavioral state of the agent (e.g., 'gathering', 'combat', 'trading', 'exploring').

lastHeartbeat.description: Timestamp of the last 'world heartbeat' tick that processed this agent, indicating its last active state.

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Defines a quest within the game, which can be dynamically generated by AI or pre-defined, offering objectives and rewards to players.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest's storyline and objectives.

type.description: The category or type of the quest (e.g., 'main', 'side', 'daily', 'generated').

status.description: The current status of the quest (e.g., 'active', 'completed', 'failed', 'available').

rewardItemIds.description: References to GameItems given as rewards upon quest completion. (Relationship: GameItem N:N Quest)

requiredItemIds.description: References to GameItems required to start or complete the quest. (Relationship: GameItem N:N Quest)

generatedByAI.description: Indicates if this quest was dynamically generated by the AI-powered content system.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the quest was created or made available.

completionDate.description: Timestamp when the quest was completed by a player (optional, only set upon completion).

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Represents a Non-Player Character within the game world, potentially with a unique, AI-generated personality and associated quests.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

personalityDescription.description: A detailed description of the NPC's personality and characteristics, often dynamically generated by AI.

location.description: The current location of the NPC in the game world.

associatedQuestIds.description: References to Quests that are directly associated with this NPC. (Relationship: Quest N:N NPC)

GameItem.title: GameItem
GameItem.description: Defines an item that exists within the game, which can be collected, used, traded, or purchased.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameItem entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the item and its properties or lore.

type.description: The category or type of the item (e.g., 'weapon', 'armor', 'resource', 'currency', 'consumable').

value.description: The in-game currency value of the item.

weight.description: The weight of the item, impacting inventory capacity.

primaryAssetId.description: Reference to the primary GameAsset entity representing this item's visual model or texture. (Relationship: GameAsset 1:N GameItem)

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records details of a purchase made by a user through the secure Axiom Storefront, including items bought and payment information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that initiated this transaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The total monetary amount of the transaction.

currency.description: The currency in which the transaction was made (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the transaction (e.g., 'PayPal').

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'completed', 'pending', 'failed').

purchasedItemIds.description: References to GameItems purchased in this transaction. (Relationship: GameItem N:N Transaction)

GameAsset.title: GameAsset
GameAsset.description: Manages references to in-game assets and textures stored in Firebase Storage, allowing dynamic downloading and assignment to 3D models.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameAsset entity.

assetName.description: A unique, descriptive name for the asset (e.g., 'ship_model_v1', 'asteroid_texture').

storagePath.description: The URI path to the asset's file in Firebase Storage.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the asset file (e.g., 'image/png', 'model/gltf').

version.description: The version identifier for the asset, useful for updates and caching (e.g., '1.0.0').

description.description: A brief description of what the asset represents or is used for.

uploadedDate.description: Timestamp when the asset file was uploaded.

definition.description: Stores core player profiles. Access is restricted to the owner matching `userProfileId` from the path, or administrators. The document ID (`userProfileId`) matches `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Contains details for individual player agents owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' for authorization independence, allowing rules to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId` directly without parent document lookups.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the owner's user profile (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: playerAgentId
params.description: The unique ID for the specific player agent.

definition.description: Records secure storefront transactions for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' for authorization independence, enabling rules to verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId` directly.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile that initiated the transaction (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID for the transaction record.

definition.description: Tracks a specific player agent's progress on a global quest. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' and 'playerAgentId' for authorization independence. The document ID (`questId`) matches the global quest's ID.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the owner's user profile (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: playerAgentId
params.description: The unique ID for the specific player agent tracking quest progress.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique ID of the global Quest entity this progress refers to.

definition.description: Stores global game configuration settings. Readable by all authenticated users (game clients), but writeable only by administrators or authorized Cloud Functions.

params.name: settingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific game world setting.

definition.description: Defines global quest content and objectives. Readable by all authenticated users; writeable only by administrators or AI-powered Cloud Functions.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific quest definition.

definition.description: Defines Non-Player Characters (NPCs). Readable by all authenticated users; writeable only by administrators or AI-powered Cloud Functions.

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific NPC.

definition.description: Defines static properties of all in-game items. Readable by all authenticated users; writeable only by administrators or game logic Cloud Functions.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific game item definition.

definition.description: Manages references to game assets stored in Firebase Storage. Readable by all authenticated users (for client-side dynamic downloading); writeable only by administrators or asset management processes.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific game asset.

definition.description: A collection used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document with a user's UID in this collection grants administrative privileges across the application. Only administrators can modify this collection.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of a user granted administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Axiom Frontier is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering strictly to the core design principles. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

For user-specific and hierarchical data such as `UserProfile`, `PlayerAgent`, and `Transaction`, authorization is achieved without relying on `get()` operations in security rules. This is critical for preventing security rule waterfall reads, enabling atomic batch writes, and simplifying debugging. 

*   `UserProfile` documents are stored under `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}` where `userProfileId` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. This path-based ownership is the most secure and straightforward pattern for private user data. Rules can simply check `request.auth.uid == userProfileId` from the path. Admin access is granted via a separate `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection.
*   `PlayerAgent` documents are nested under their owning `UserProfile` at `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/playerAgents/{playerAgentId}`. To ensure Authorization Independence, each `PlayerAgent` document explicitly includes the `userProfileId` field. Security rules will verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId` (from the document itself) in conjunction with `request.auth.uid == userProfileId` (from the path). This denormalization means rules do not need to `get()` the parent `UserProfile` document to determine ownership, thereby maintaining atomicity and efficiency.
*   Similarly, `Transaction` documents are nested under `UserProfile` at `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/transactions/{transactionId}` and also contain a denormalized `userProfileId` field. This allows rules to authorize access based solely on the document's content and the request's authentication context (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId`), aligning with the Authorization Independence principle.
*   The inferred `PlayerQuestProgress` entity (to track agent-specific quest progress) follows the same pattern, nested under `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/playerAgents/{playerAgentId}/questProgress/{questId}` and including `userProfileId` and `playerAgentId` within the document for direct rule evaluation.

**QAPs (Queries as a Privilege):**

The structure facilitates secure `list` operations (QAPs) by strictly adhering to Structural Segregation:

*   **User-Private Data:** Collections like `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/playerAgents` and `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/transactions` are inherently secured by the path structure. A user can only query their own subcollections because the `userProfileId` wildcard in the path will match their `request.auth.uid`. Any query attempting to access another user's subcollection will fail due to rule constraints, thus preventing accidental data exposure and supporting QAPs.
*   **Global, Read-Only Data:** Collections such as `/gameWorldSettings`, `/quests`, `/npcs`, `/gameItems`, and `/gameAssets` are designed to be globally readable by all authenticated users (e.g., the game client). Since all documents within these collections share the same security posture (read-all, write-admin/Cloud Function), `list` operations are secure and efficient. Rules can explicitly grant read access to `request.auth != null`, and write access to `isAdmin()` (checking existence in `/roles_admin/{userId}`). This clear segregation ensures that global queries do not inadvertently return sensitive data, as no such data exists in these collections.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**

*   **Private Data:** All personal user data (profile, agents, transactions) is grouped under `/userProfiles/{userId}`, providing clear path-based ownership.
*   **Global Content:** Game definitions (`Quest`, `NPC`, `GameItem`, `GameAsset`) and global settings (`GameWorldSetting`) reside in top-level collections, signifying their global accessibility (read-only for players, write-only for admins/Cloud Functions).
*   **DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):** A dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{userId}` is used for role-based access control. The mere existence of a document for a `userId` in this collection grants administrative privileges across the application, making roles explicit and easily auditable directly in the database, avoiding custom claims and relying solely on `request.auth.uid`.

This comprehensive design minimizes complexity in security rules, maximizes debuggability, and aligns with best practices for a scalable and secure Firebase application.
